summary: 本轮检索发现 1 项新的高相关条目：一篇面向共体式可穿戴机器人手的变量自主权方法论文。它把人机共享单一物理身体、在不同任务阶段切换自主等级作为核心贡献，且包含真实用户研究与量化结果，因此属于本轮最值得跟踪的新增进展。
summary_en: This run found 1 newly relevant item: a paper on variable autonomy for a co-embodied wearable robotic hand. It makes human-robot co-embodiment over a single physical body the central contribution, and it includes a real user study with quantitative results, so it is the most important new item to track in this run.

items.title: One Body, Two Minds: Variable Autonomy Approach for a Co-embodied Robotic Hand
items.authors_or_org: Piotr Koczy, Yuchong Zhang, Danica Kragic, Michael C. Welle
items.relevance: 直接围绕可穿戴灵巧机器人手的共体控制与变量自主权展开，灵巧手本体与操控方式都是核心贡献。
items.relevance_en: It is centered on co-embodied control for a wearable dexterous robotic hand, so the hand itself and its autonomy policy are the core contribution.
items.novelty_signal: 2026-06-24 首发的 arXiv 预印本，且公开摘要包含真实用户研究和明确的任务级定量结果。
items.novelty_signal_en: A newly surfaced arXiv preprint from 2026-06-24, with a real user study and task-level quantitative results in the public abstract.
items.analysis_zh: 这项工作的主要贡献是提出“共体式变量自主权”框架，把人和机器人放进同一个物理身体里协同工作，而不是传统共享自主那样分属不同平台。它要解决的是辅助式灵巧操作里“全自动会削弱用户主导权、全手动又增加认知负担”的矛盾，因此方法上采用基于示教的视觉运动扩散策略，在已知物体附近实现自主抓取；一旦抓住工具，系统再通过头部手势把执行权交回给人类，并始终保留释放手势作为人工接管通道。公开摘要给出了真实用户研究：44 名参与者完成五类双手任务，完成时间在多次试验中提升 23.3%，最佳策略变体任务成功率达到 93.6%，总体印象和日常使用意愿评分也较高。总体来说，这篇工作把“一个身体、两个心智”的共体理念落到了可穿戴机器人手上，证明变量自主权在辅助机器人场景中可行；限制在于摘要尚未完整说明失败案例、对更复杂工具与更长时程任务的泛化，以及对不同用户群体的稳定性。
items.analysis_en: The main contribution is a variable-autonomy framework for a co-embodied wearable robotic hand, putting human and robot inside a single physical body rather than splitting control across separate agents. It addresses a core assistive-robotics trade-off: full autonomy can reduce user agency, while full manual control imposes continuous cognitive load. Methodologically, the system uses a learning-from-demonstration visuomotor diffusion policy to autonomously grasp known objects when the user places the hand near them, then hands execution back to the human via head gestures for tool actuation, while preserving a release gesture for human veto at all times. The public abstract reports a user study with 44 participants completing five bimanual tasks, 23.3% faster completion across trials, a best-policy task success rate of 93.6%, and strong acceptance ratings. Overall, the paper makes a concrete case that “one body, two minds” is viable for assistive robotics; the limitation is that the public information still does not fully expose failure modes, generalization to more complex tools or longer-horizon tasks, or robustness across user populations.

caveats: 本轮优先检索 arXiv 与原始项目页，严格按灵巧机器人手、灵巧操作、共体式控制和变量自主权筛选。
公开摘要已给出用户研究与定量结果，因此本条分析相对更有把握；但失败案例、泛化与鲁棒性仍需后续全文确认。
本轮仅新增 1 项高相关条目，已同步更新日报与历史状态文件。
